steps.title: Spot the sentence boundary
steps.content: The text contains two independent clauses at the point of the blank: one clause ends with “dependent on these adjustments,” and another begins with “Prior to VisiCalc's release, changing a paper spreadsheet often required redoing the entire sheet by hand.”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A period provides the required punctuation. The other choices fail:
  • A creates a fused sentence with no separation
  • B uses only a comma (not sufficient here)
  • D uses the ungrammatical phrase “and prior”
. That matches choice C — C is correct.
